ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sections" (
  "section_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each section */,
  "section_name" TEXT /* Name of the section (e.g., Print Engine, Client Application) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the section */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the section was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the section was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("section_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "section_id" INTEGER /* ID of the section being tested */,
  "total_cases" INTEGER /* Total number of test cases */,
  "not_tested" INTEGER /* Number of cases not tested */,
  "failed" INTEGER /* Number of failed test cases */,
  "passed" INTEGER /* Number of passed test cases */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date when the test was conducted */,
  "test_run_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the test run that generated this result */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the test result */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the result was recorded */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the result was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_section_id FOREIGN KEY ("section_id") REFERENCES sections ("section_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_run_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_run_id") REFERENCES test_runs ("run_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_cases" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case */,
  "section_id" INTEGER /* ID of the section the test case belongs to */,
  "case_name" TEXT /* Name of the test case */,
  "description" TEXT /* A brief description of the test case */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the test case (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the test case (e.g., pending, in progress, completed) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test case was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test case was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_section_id FOREIGN KEY ("section_id") REFERENCES sections ("section_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, tester, QA engineer) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_runs" (
  "run_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test run */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test case being run */,
  "run_date" TEXT /* Date the test was run */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the test run (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who ran the test */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the test was run (e.g., development, staging, production) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test run was initiated */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test run was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("run_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_runs_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES test_cases ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_runs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test result being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the test result */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the test result was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the test result */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the test result */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the access event occurred */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES test_results ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
